prompt: Add a hook to fetch the vehicle list from the fleet service so the Vehicles page can render it with status filters
expected_avoid: native fetch() (repo uses axios via per-resource service modules in src/services/api/*.ts)
assuming top-level pagination (nest-fleet-service has pagination NESTED inside data!)
assuming numeric status field (nest-fleet uses STRING 'Success'|'Failed')
expected_use: axios via the existing per-resource service module pattern (src/services/api/vehicles.ts)
Bearer token from crossDomainStorage helper, 401 refresh+retry queue is shared infra
envelope: { status: 'Success'|'Failed', data: { items, pagination }, error?, errors? } — pagination is INSIDE data for nest-fleet-service
BaseAuthType WEB (admin) identity on the auth side
React Router DOM v7 useNavigate for redirects
expected_pattern_ref: Adaptation Layer — react-fleet-management-web data fetch (rules L431); Per-service envelope nest-fleet-service nested pagination (rules L500, L623); Backend dependency nest-fleet (rules L445)
context_notes: CRITICAL: nest-fleet-service uses BaseApiResponse<T> with STRING status, and pagination is NESTED inside data. nest-express-service has the SAME class name BaseApiResponse<T> but numeric status and top-level pagination — discriminate by SERVICE, not class name. Code that reads response.pagination at top level will be undefined here.
